title: Influence of Triton X-100 on the PCR–profiles of enzyme genes and isozyme patterns in Beta vulgaris L. during germination
author: Vinichenko N.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The purpose of this work is to study the effect of the detergent Triton X-100 on the PCR–profiles of enzyme genes in germinating beet seeds (Beta vulgaris L.). In this study, we used hybrid seeds obtained by crossing sugar and red table beets. PCR–profiles of enzyme genes in the control and experimental (treated with 0.1% Triton X-100 solution) seed groups were compared from the 18th hour to the 114th hour of germination with an interval of 24 hours. In addition, in ungerminated seeds, both the PCR–profiles of enzyme genes and the isoenzyme spectra determined by these genes were analyzed too. TX-100 influences ontogenetic changes in the Gpi and Me PCR–profiles differently. It can be assumed that TX-100 induces changes in the membrane–associated structures regulated processes. The dual effect of detergent TX-100 on the enzyme genes PCR–profiles and gene expression indicates that TX-100 makes its primary impact on the genome.
